comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: de012989ea1a0453af169a9534d8b82f74d3a2b9754961e72fb6efada9ad9af3 -->

**Location:** `src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:85-106`
**Violated invariant:** Every diagnostic that writes side effects must provide a cleanup mechanism or be idempotent; timing assumptions must be poll-based rather than hard-coded delays.
**Proof:** `rg 'importBibTeXToZotero' src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts -A 20 | head -25`
**Pattern:** side-effect-without-cleanup + hard-coded-timing
**Why this matters:** Running the diagnostic multiple times accumulates unstoppable stale items in the user's Zotero library. Under I/O load or slow storage, the 1500ms window may be insufficient, causing a false diagnostic failure that the user cannot distinguish from a genuine Zotero connectivity problem.
**Evidence:** `src/server/liveDiagnostics.ts:85-106` (file-read)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [Slop Review][tier1] SLOP
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 414a801a10b0bf1b66d0d860249959e2a0b9a02fd0b3346efd05ca77047134b8 -->

**Location:** `src/columnModel.ts:177-178`
**Violated invariant:** A value crossing from an untyped framework boundary (TanStack columnId: string) into a typed owned domain (SortKey union) must be validated at the boundary, not cast with a bare TypeScript `as` assertion that evaporates at runtime.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'as SortKey' src/columnModel.ts && tsc --noEmit --strict src/columnModel.ts 2>&1 | head -20`
**Pattern:** type-assertion-bypass: A TanStack `columnId: string` is asserted to `SortKey` with an unguarded `as` cast. The `sortableValue` function that receives this value branches on specific `SortKey` members; an unrecognized key from persistence or a new column definition passes the cast silently and returns `undefined`, producing `NaN` subtraction in the comparator.
**Why this matters:** A persistent column layout from a future version, or a programmatic column registration that bypasses the `SortKey` union, silently breaks sorting: every comparison returns `0` (no sort), hiding the fault behind normal-looking but unsorted data.
**Evidence:** `src/columnModel.ts:172-186` (file-read)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [Slop Review][tier2] SLOP SUSPECT
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 91fdc2d6f3c73f11e9636f3954cf3f1181e0c8625841faab3c68524b2d20c76d -->

**Location:** `src/App.tsx:120-134`
**Violated invariant:** A user-facing data export must not bundle internal rendering metadata (column visibility, order, sizing) with domain data (Zotero items, collections) in the same serialized artifact, because the column layout is an implementation detail of the table component, not a property of the Zotero library.
**Proof:** `grep -A 15 'exportDatabaseJson' src/App.tsx | grep -E '(columnVisibility|columnOrder|columnSizing|columns:)'`
**Pattern:** domain-contamination: The `exportDatabaseJson` handler serializes TanStack table state (`{ columnVisibility, columnOrder, columnSizing }`) alongside `{ items, collections }` into a single downloadable JSON. Column layout is a rendering concern; bundling it with domain data creates an artifact where column widths and hidden-state flags appear to be Zotero metadata.
**Why this matters:** The exported artifact is not a clean Zotero data snapshot — it is a snapshot of one browser's rendering state welded onto the library data. An import/restore function would need to strip or ignore the `columns` key, and any downstream consumer that reads this file must know the column keys are internal TanStack identifiers, not Zotero field names.
**Evidence:** `src/App.tsx:120-134` (file-read)

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

Every non-synthetic collection from the repository must carry a Zotero collection key at the import boundary, but both the runtime schema (CollectionSchema) and the type interface (Collection) declare key as optional, so a future schema change that drops key would not be caught at compile time.

## ai-review/general / A request with a malformed JSON body sent to any POST endpoint must return HTTP 400 with kind invalid_request, not HTTP 500 with kind internal_error. The error middleware must distinguish client-level

Resolver execution failures (timeout, process crash, empty output, invalid BibTeX) must not be classified as 'upstream_boundary_failed' because they are local executor failures, not upstream Zotero write-plugin failures. The error kind 'upstream_boundary_failed' must identify Zotero connectivity/write-plugin errors only; resolver execution failures must produce a distinct kind so monitoring and diagnostics can distinguish the two semantically different failure domains.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/143)

## ai-review/general / A package name must appear in at most one dependency category. Having `vite` in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` creates an ambiguous declaration: npm, yarn, and pnpm each resolve this differ

A package name must appear in at most one dependency category. Having `vite` in both `dependencies` and `devDependencies` creates an ambiguous declaration: npm, yarn, and pnpm each resolve this differently, and `npm install --production` installs a build tool as a runtime dependency.

comments.body:
## ai-review/general / Route-level tests must exercise the production code path (loadLibraryFromDatabaseUri -> assertZoteroDatabaseContract -> queryLibrary), not bypass the contract preflight by passing queryLibrary(db) dir

Item types should be validated exactly once against a definitive contract. Calling parseItemType during preflight (line 230) and then LibraryPayloadSchema.parse (line 564) on the same data duplicates the same validation without providing a distinct fault domain.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/141)

comments.author: github-advanced-security
comments.body:
## ai-review/general / A runtime-validated string value cast with `as ItemType` must match the TypeScript ItemType union exactly. The guard `ITEM_TYPES.includes(value)` and the `as ItemType` cast are independent code paths 

A runtime-validated string value cast with `as ItemType` must match the TypeScript ItemType union exactly. The guard `ITEM_TYPES.includes(value)` and the `as ItemType` cast are independent code paths that can drift: adding a type to ITEM_TYPES without adding it to the TypeScript union (or vice versa) makes the type annotation incorrect without a build error.

[Show more details](https://github.com/dzackgarza/zotero-gui/security/code-scanning/137)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [General Review][tier1] SQL NOT IN subquery NULL sensitivity — entire library vanishes on DB corruption
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 283075c6373bbe678e1c6c8786dfd87208e615b8de6e089939c363aba9b0a04a -->

**Location:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:409-415`
**Violated invariant:** All non-child items must be queryable regardless of NULL presence in subquery result sets. x NOT IN (subquery) is UNKNOWN (not TRUE) when subquery contains NULL, filtering out every row.
**Proof:** `sqlite3 :memory: 'CREATE TABLE t(x); INSERT INTO t VALUES (1),(2),(NULL); SELECT 1 WHERE 3 NOT IN (SELECT x FROM t);'`
**Symptom:** Four SQL queries in zoteroRepository.ts use `WHERE i.itemID NOT IN (SELECT itemID FROM ...)`. If a corrupted Zotero DB or future schema change produces a NULL `itemID` in any subquery result set, SQL semantics dictate that `NOT IN (subquery_with_null)` returns FALSE for every row, making the query return zero rows. The entire library silently disappears.
**Source:** Lines 409-415 (items query), 444-445 (notes query), 462 (attachments query), 482 (standalone attachments query).
**Consequence:** A single NULL `itemID` in `itemAttachments`, `itemNotes`, or `itemAnnotations` makes the entire top-level item list empty. The app renders 'My Library (0 items)', no error is surfaced, and the user sees an empty library with no diagnostic.
**Evidence:** `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:409-415` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:444-445` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:462-462` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:482-482` (source)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [General Review][tier1] Double Zod parse of library payload on attachment-open
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: 737ce9ca4f76b6578d1ab951045529b205b6ac479de495bdf679ee816abc4ad1 -->

**Location:** `src/server/server.ts:229-229`
**Violated invariant:** A validated LibraryPayload must not be parsed through the same Zod schema a second time — the return type of loadValidatedLibrary is already LibraryPayload.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'LibraryPayloadSchema.parse' src/server/server.ts src/server/zoteroRepository.ts`
**Symptom:** The POST /api/attachments/:id/open handler parses the entire library payload through Zod twice: once inside `deps.loadLibrary()` (which calls `loadValidatedLibrary` -> `LibraryPayloadSchema.parse` at zoteroRepository.ts:603) and again via the explicit `LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary())` at server.ts:229.
**Source:** src/server/server.ts:229 — `const attachment = requireAttachmentWithPath(LibraryPayloadSchema.parse(deps.loadLibrary()), attachmentId);`
**Consequence:** Every attachment-open request validates the full library payload twice. For a 50k-item library with 5 field values each, ~250k Zod field validations run twice per request.
**Evidence:** `src/server/server.ts:229-229` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:603-603` (source), `src/server/zoteroRepository.ts:353-366` (source)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [General Review][tier1] Regex pattern recompiled on every from-source POST
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c77dafebbed4707c504e0959db3bef4f5529cb9f2260d3593091c03a408854d5 -->

**Location:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137-137`
**Violated invariant:** Regex patterns that are fixed at configuration load time must be compiled once, not on every HTTP request.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'new RegExp' src/server/resolverPlugins.ts`
**Symptom:** `pluginAcceptsInput` compiles `new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, 'i')` on every invocation. With 4 plugins and ~5 patterns each, every POST /api/items/from-source request compiles 20 regex objects from scratch.
**Source:** src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137 — `return plugin.acceptedInputs.some(descriptor => new RegExp(descriptor.pattern, 'i').test(input.trim()));`
**Consequence:** Unnecessary regex compilation on every request. Patterns are validated at load time (line 121) but the compiled objects are discarded. If a pattern is invalid, the error surfaces inside a per-request path instead of failing loud at startup.
**Evidence:** `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:121-121` (source), `src/server/resolverPlugins.ts:137-137` (source), `src/server/server.ts:189-189` (source)

comments.author: github-actions
comments.body:
### [General Review][tier1] Shared mutable importMode across tests creates cascading-failure risk
<!-- ai-review-fingerprint: c9fdac7211ae88b0b50e8b954ceae251588e25f326a105ca47ce4cf5418f6be2 -->

**Location:** `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19-19`
**Violated invariant:** Test state must not be shared across tests via mutable module-level variables. Each test must fully control its own fault-injection conditions.
**Proof:** `grep -n 'importMode' src/server/serverErrors.test.ts`
**Symptom:** `let importMode: 'fail' | 'success' = 'fail'` is a module-level mutable variable shared by all tests in the describe block, controlling whether a shared import HTTP server returns success or failure.
**Source:** src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19 — `let importMode: 'fail' | 'success' = 'fail';`
**Consequence:** If a test fails after changing `importMode`, the next test inherits the stale mode, producing cascading failures that are hard to diagnose. Also prevents parallel test execution.
**Evidence:** `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:19-19` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:142-176` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:267-267` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:341-341` (source), `src/server/serverErrors.test.ts:350-350` (source)
